P0.name: Critical
declareWhenAny: confirmed or credible cross-game data exposure
credential, session-token, token-hash, or service-secret exposure
authorization bypass or unauthorized economic mutation
unbounded or actively repeating economic writes
irrecoverable data loss or inability to contain a destructive write path
active compromise of production control or deployment authority
automaticStopConditions: disable the affected route, feature, worker, or deployment when required for containment
move classrooms to documented read-only fallback

P1.name: High
declareWhenAny: bounded economic corruption within one or more games
widespread inability to sign in or use a required classroom workflow
runaway market, story, reward, or notification execution with contained scope
failure of Admin pause or correction controls
material integrity risk with no confirmed cross-game or credential exposure
automaticStopConditions: pause the affected capability or games
prevent retries that could duplicate writes
switch affected classrooms to documented fallback

P2.name: Moderate
declareWhenAny: major workflow degradation with a safe workaround
bounded incorrect calculation or stale read with no ongoing harmful writes
partial service outage that does not require classroom-wide stop

P3.name: Low
declareWhenAny: localized presentation, reporting, or noncritical workflow defect
minor operational issue with no security, privacy, integrity, or classroom continuity impact

forbiddenActions: direct production database correction as a normal path
deleting or rewriting append-only ledger history
reusing a failed write with a new idempotency key before outcome verification
placing credentials, session tokens, internal UUIDs, player names, or exploit details in public issues
declaring recovery before authoritative read and invariant verification

closureRequirements: root cause or bounded causal hypothesis is recorded
authoritative state and economic invariants pass
temporary access and mitigations are removed or time-bounded
corrective actions have owners and due dates
post-incident review is scheduled for P0 and P1
